item.title: Mayor's Office of Contract Services — Special Materials: LL63/DOHMH/EXT
item.description:
Outcome: Special Materials

Notice of Intent to Renew or Amend Contract(s) Not Included in FY2026 Annual Contracting Plan and Schedule 

NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that the Mayor will be entering into the following renewal(s)/amendment(s)) of (a) contract(s) not included in the FY 2026 Annual Contracting Plan and Schedule that is published pursuant to New York City Charter § 312(a): 

Agency: DOHMH

Vendor:  Fund for Public Health in New York

Description of Services to be Provided: Fund for Public Health in New York  (FPHNY) will work with the Department to develop and execute

item.title: Youth and Community Development — Procurement: Summer Youth Employment
item.description: Outcome: Procurement

item.title: Citywide Administrative Services — Procurement: G2G for CFTP/SFTP/VR Operation
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement

Pursuant to Section 3-13 of the Procurement Policy Board Rules, the New York City Department of Citywide Administrative Services ('DCAS') intends to enter into a contract with the US Department of Transportation Volpe Center to procure via Government to Government purchase services to produce Clean Fleet Transition Plan (CFTP) and a Safe Fleet Transition Plan (SFTP) reports. The effort is structured around several key areas aimed at increasing the safety and sustainability of vehicles operated in the City of New York in accordance with the Safe System Approach, Vision Zero, and minimizing

item.description:

item.description:
Outcome: Procurement

School: PS 108 (Bronx)

Location: 1166 Neill Avenue, Bronx NY 10461

item.title: Citywide Administrative Services — Procurement: SLS ESRI ARCGIS Mapping Software license Continuation FY26
item.description: Outcome: Procurement

item.title: Mayor's Office of Criminal Justice — Procurement: Brooklyn Defender Services FY27 Trials NAE (QNS)
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement

The Mayor s Office of Criminal Justice (MOCJ) intends to enter into a Negotiated Acquisition Extension (NAE) with Brooklyn Defenders Services. 

In New York State, localities are statutorily and constitutionally required to fund legal defense for criminal cases for those who cannot afford private representation. Vendor is contracted by the City to provide both homicide and non-homicide trial level criminal indigent defense representation services. This is to extend the existing contract 1 year to allow time for a long term procurement to be put in place.

item.title: Mayor's Office of Contract Services — Special Materials: LL63/DOHMH/NEW
item.description:
Outcome: Special Materials

Notice of Intent to Issue New Solicitation(s) Not Included in FY 2026 Annual Contracting Plan and Schedule

Agency:  DOHMH

Description of Services to be Provided: The Vendor will develop funding sources, engage in fund-raising activities in the public and private sector and apply for, receive, administer and expend donations, gifts and bequest

item.title: Citywide Administrative Services — Procurement: Lane Separator System Flexible Delineators - GSA - DOT
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement

DOT GSA Intergov procurement for Lane Separator System Flexible Delineators. (Federal GSA Contract #GS-07F-193GA. Suppliers wishing to be considered for a contract with the General Services Administration of the Federal Government are advised to contact the GSA National Customer Service Center via email at: mashelpdesk@gsa.gov, or by phone at: 800-488-3111).

item.title: Mayor's Office of Contract Services — Special Materials: LL63/Comptroller/NEW

Description of Services to be Provided: Request for Proposal for Specialty Consultant

Anticipated Procurement Method: Request for Proposal

Anticipated Contract Start Date: 7/1/2027

Anticipated Contr

item.title: Design and Construction — Procurement: SEK200387- INSTALLATION OF TIDE GATE CHAMBER ON 9 -6 X 5 0 SEWER AT AVENUE K
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement

Responses to this CSB must be submitted via PASSPort. To access the solicitation, vendors should visit the PASSPort Public Portal at the following website: https://passport.cityofnewyork.us/page.aspx/en/rfp/request_browse_public. Click on the Search Funding Opportunities in PASSPort blue box. This will take you to the Public Portal of all procurements in the PASSPort system. To quickly locate the CSB, insert the EPIN (85026B0082) into the Keywords search field. Please note, this link is only for NON-PQL projects. For PQL projects, only certified vendors will receive the solicitations.

item.title: Administrative Trials and Hearings — Procurement: ATAS - AIMS Integration. Net Developer
item.description: Outcome: Procurement

item.title: Dept. of Social Svcs/Human Resources Administration — Procurement: Home Care Services Program Citywide
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement

The services would be provided to Medicaid-eligible individuals who are medically and/or physically disabled and/or frail and elderly. Additionally, stability would be maintained and better service would be provided due to the continuity of care through these contracts. This will also allow HRA/HCSP time to focus on the vendors adherence to the New York State regulations, contract monitoring, performance evaluations, and implementing compliance indicators with NYSDOH regulatory changes. Be

item.title: Mayor's Office of Contract Services — Special Materials: LL63/DEP/NEW
item.description:
Outcome: Special Materials

Notice of Intent to Issue New Solicitation(s) Not Included in FY 2026 Annual Contracting Plan and Schedule

NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that the mayor will be issuing the following solicitation(s) not included in the FY 2026 Annual Contracting Plan and Schedule that is published pursuant to New York City Charter § 312(a):

Agency: Department of Environmental Protection

Description of services to be provided:  CSO-GC-SFS-CM-OH-CP3: Construction Management Services for the Superstructure of the Owls Head (OH-007) Combined Sewer Overflow Facility at the Gowan

item.title: Health and Mental Hygiene — Procurement: Recovery Center- Negotiated Acquisition
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement

In Accordance with 3-04(b)(2) (ii) of the Procurement Board (PPB) Rules, the NYC Department of Health is seeking to enter into a Negotiated Acquisition (limited pool) with New York Therapeutic for Office of Addiction Services and Supports (OASAS)-licensed Recovery Center.

The aim is to enhance non-clinical recovery support, including emotional, informational, instrumental (concrete) support, and positive affiliation. The recovery center is funded by NYS OASAS; there is a limited number of vendors available and able to perform the work. NYC Health currently holds contracts with eight

item.description:
Outcome: Public Comment on Contract Awards

This is a notice that NYC Department of Health and Mental Hygiene is seeking comments from the public about the proposed contract below.  

 

Contract Type: General Contract (CT1)

Contractor:  WellLife Network Inc.

Contractor Address: 142-02 20th Avenue, 3rd Floor, Flushing, NY 11351

Scope of Services: To provide program supp

item.title: Sanitation — Procurement: Fulcrum Enterprise Software Renewal
item.description: Outcome: Procurement

item.title: Dept. of Social Svcs/Human Resources Administration — Special Materials: Community Guardian Services Concept Paper - 06926Y0421
item.description:
Outcome: Special Materials

Adult Protective Services (APS), a program of the New York City Human Resources Administration (HRA), is committed to enhancing its policies and procedures to more effectively serve Article 81 court appointed community guardian clients. DSS/HRA issues this Concept Paper regarding a forthcoming procurement for Community Guardianship Program (CGP) services pursuant to Article 81 of the New York State Mental Hygiene Law.

item.title: School Construction Authority — Procurement: Flood Elimination/Parapets/Roofs
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement

Pre-Bid Walk through Date and Time:  May 8, 2026 at 11:30AM at:  420 Union Street, Brooklyn, NY 11231.

Potential bidders are encouraged to attend but this walkthrough is not mandatory. Meet at the Custodian s Office.

Bid Opening Date and Time: May 20, 2026 at 12:30PM.


All bidders must be prequalified at the t

item.title: Chief Medical Examiner — Procurement: Biomek i5 Span-8 and Beckman Biomek i7 Hybrid (MS + Span 8) with enclosures
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement

The Contractor shall provide the OCME with, installation, training, on-site preventative maintenance and repair services for one (1) Biomek i5 Span-8 with enclosure system, and one (1) Biomek i7 Hybrid (MS + Span 8) with enclosure and related accessories ( Equipment ). The Equipment is used for all aspects of liquid handling of DNA samples and specific reagents, including pipetting, dilution, dispensing, and integration. Any other ancillary equipment or accessories acquired by OCME shall be serviced and maintained in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.

item.title: Housing Authority — Procurement: SMPD_MATERIALS_FOR_MASONRY TRADE- PLASTER, LIME & THOROSEAL
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement

The New York City Housing Authority ( NYCHA ), Supply Management and Procurement Department ( SMP ), through this Solicitation, seeks bids from qualified vendors to provide NYCHA with materials for SMPD_Materials_for_Masonry Trade at Various Developments located on all five (5) boroughs of New York City. The materials to be provided by the successful vendor are described in greater detail in the RFQ Number: 521188. Interested vendors are invited to obtain a copy of the opportunity at NYCHA s website by going t

item.title: Mayor's Office of Criminal Justice — Procurement: Legal Aid Society FY27 Trials NAE
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement

The Mayor s Office of Criminal Justice (MOCJ) intends to enter into a Negotiated Acquisition Extension (NAE) with The Legal Aid Society. 

In New York State, localities are statutorily and constitutionally required to fund legal defense for criminal cases for those who cannot afford private representation. Vendor is contracted by the City to provide both homicide and non-homicide trial level criminal indigent defense representation services. This is to extend the existing contract 1 year to allow time for a long term procurement to be put in place.

Outcome: Procurement

The Mayor s Office of Criminal Justice (MOCJ) intends to enter into a Negotiated Acquisition Extension (NAE) with The Bronx Defenders. 

In New York State, localities are statutorily and constitutionally required to fund legal defense for criminal cases for those who cannot afford private representation. Vendor is contracted by the City to provide both homicide and non-homicide trial level criminal indigent defense representation services. This is to extend the existing contract 1 year to allow time for a long term procurement to be put in place.

item.title: Mayor's Office of Criminal Justice — Procurement: Neighborhood Defender Service FY27 Trials NAE
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement

The Mayor s Office of Criminal Justice (MOCJ) intends to enter into a Negotiated Acquisition Extension (NAE) with Neighborhood Defender Services Inc.

In New York State, localities are statutorily and constitutionally required to fund legal defense for criminal cases for those who cannot afford private representation. Vendor is contracted by the City to provide both homicide and non-homicide trial level criminal indigent defense representation services. This is to extend the existing contract 1 year to allow time for a long term procurement to be put in place.

item.title: Small Business Services — Procurement: FY26 Business Solutions Upper Manhattan/Washington Heights
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement

NYC Business Solutions is a suite of services funded by the Federal Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act ( WIOA ). The Centers help entrepreneurs and small businesses to start, operate and grow in New York City. Services are offered at no cost and can help businesses of any size and at any stage. 

The negotiated acquisition extension will allow the agency adequate time to solicit the new RFP, make selections, finalize negotiations and process awards for this program and enable the current Business Solutions C

item.description: Outcome: Procurement

item.title: Mayor's Office of Criminal Justice — Procurement: Brooklyn Defender Services FY27 Trials NAE (BK)
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement

The Mayor s Office of Criminal Justice (MOCJ) intends to enter into a Negotiated Acquisition Extension (NAE) with Brooklyn Defender Services. 

In New York State, localities are statutorily and constitutionally required to fund legal defense for criminal cases for those who cannot afford private representation. Vendor is contracted by the City to provide both homicide and non-homicide trial level criminal indigent defense representation services. This is to extend the existing contract 1 year to allow time for a long term procurement to be put in place.

item.title: Youth and Community Development — Procurement: FY 26 Neighborhood Youth Team Sports Grant
item.description:
Outcome: Procurement

The New York State Office of Children and Family Services (OCFS) created the new fund in the state s fiscal year 2025-2026 budget to provide awards to support youth team sports programs for underserved youth under age 18. The funding supports youth development through team sports programs and would be awarded to local community-based organizations and nonprofits. Grant requires the City to have funds awarded and expensed prior to th
